claimText: Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) are registered under the Companies Act, 1956.
evidenceExcerpt: NBFCs are registered under the Companies Act, 1956
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal foundation and registration of NBFCs.
distractorHooks: Registered under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934
Registered under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949

claimText: The principal business of NBFCs involves lending and investment, but explicitly excludes agriculture, industry, and real estate activities.
evidenceExcerpt: engaging in lending and investment while excluding agriculture, industry, and real estate activities.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the core permitted and restricted activities of NBFCs.
distractorHooks: NBFCs primarily engage in agricultural financing
Real estate development is a core activity of NBFCs

claimText: Unlike commercial banks, NBFCs are not permitted to accept demand deposits.
evidenceExcerpt: Unlike banks, NBFCs cannot accept demand deposits
examUse: Comparison question between Banks and NBFCs regarding deposit acceptance.
distractorHooks: NBFCs can accept demand deposits up to a certain limit
Deposit-taking NBFCs can accept demand deposits

claimText: NBFCs do not form part of the payment and settlement system and cannot issue cheques drawn on themselves.
evidenceExcerpt: are outside the payment and settlement system, and cannot issue cheques drawn on themselves.
examUse: Statement-based question on the operational limitations of NBFCs in the financial system.
distractorHooks: NBFCs are part of the payment and settlement system
NBFCs can issue cheques drawn on themselves for loan disbursement
